headlines.title: Wendy's shares soar for a second day as retail investors pile into their new meme darling
headlines.description: The stock's advance appeared largely disconnected from company fundamentals and instead reflected a burst of social-media enthusiasm.

headlines.title: Iraq piles pressure on OPEC over quota dispute after UAE exit
headlines.description: Reuters reported that though Iraq had considered leaving OPEC, the ​current plan is to remain a member of the group while seeking a higher output quota.

headlines.title: TikTok and YouTube are reinventing sports viewership. Broadcasters are taking note
headlines.description: Leagues, teams and media networks are increasingly meeting younger fans on social media as platforms like TikTok, Roblox and YouTube capture most of their time.

headlines.title: Iran warns ships it's 'unacceptable and dangerous' to transit the Strait of Hormuz without their approval
headlines.description: The stern warning underscores Tehran's resolve to retain control over the Strait of Hormuz and to resist transits that bypass its authorization.

headlines.title: Gold hovers around $4,000, silver holds below $60 — has the shimmer worn off the precious metal rally?
headlines.description: Gold is down almost 8% so far this year, while silver has shed more than 20%.

headlines.title: Defense stocks slide again, as Germany’s naval U-turn shakes confidence in Europe’s rearmament boom
headlines.description: Rheinmetall and other European defense stocks fell for a second day, after Germany scrapped the F126 naval program, raising procurement concerns.

headlines.title: This industrial play has been a quiet winner in 2026. Why the stock has more room to go

headlines.title: Identity theft victims face 'unconscionable' IRS delays, report says
headlines.description: Victims currently face wait times of almost two years to resolve their cases, according to the National Taxpayer Advocate.

headlines.title: Oil prices erase wartime gains as more tankers transit through Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: Oil prices fell as investors bet global crude supplies will improve, as tankers stranded in the Persian Gulf for months have begun leaving the Strait of Hormuz.
